nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Alix
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Variant of   Alex  , used only as a feminine name. Its formation has probably been influenced by   Alice .

nameMeanings.name: Adèle
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From French Adèle , an ancient name popular in medieval Europe because of the fame of a 7th-century saint, a daughter of the Frankish king Dagobert II. It is of Germanic origin, from adal ‘noble’ (a short form of a two-element name such as Adelheid; see Adelaide ). It was the name of William the Conqueror's youngest daughter ( c.1062–1137 ), who became the wife of Stephen of Blois. However, it died out in England in the later Middle Ages. It was revived in the 19th century, being the name of a character in Johann Strauss's opera Die Fledermaus. Its popularity was further reinforced in the 1930s as the name of a character in the novels of Dornford Yates .

en.lifeSummary: Alix Adèle de LUZARCHES was born from about 1130 to 1140, in La Queue-en-Brie, Val-de-Marne, Île-de-France, France. She married Mathieu II de Beaumont, Comte de Beaumont-sur-Oise about 1154.

fr.lifeSummary: Alix Adèle de LUZARCHES est née du environ 1130 au 1140, à La Queue-en-Brie, Val-de-Marne, Île-de-France, France. Elle a épousé Mathieu II de Beaumont, Comte de Beaumont-sur-Oise environ 1154.

de.lifeSummary: Alix Adèle de LUZARCHES wurde von um 1130 bis 1140 in La Queue-en-Brie, Val-de-Marne, Île-de-France, Frankreich geboren. Sie hat um 1154 Mathieu II de Beaumont, Comte de Beaumont-sur-Oise geheiratet.
